wicker_basket.name: wicker basket
wicker_basket.description: A tight-woven willow basket with a hinged lid. Equip and left-click within 15 studs of a Rabbit, Bird, or Owl to catch it. The basket becomes occupied; equip and left-click again to release the animal.

wooden_cage.name: wooden cage
wooden_cage.description: Lashed-timber cage with a drop-gate. Equip and left-click within 15 studs of a Cow, Sheep, Deer, or Red Fox to catch it. Equip and left-click to release.

wicker_basket_rabbit.name: wicker basket (Rabbit)
wicker_basket_rabbit.purpose: A basket with a rabbit inside
wicker_basket_rabbit.description: A wicker basket carrying a Rabbit. Equip and left-click to release the animal at your feet.

wicker_basket_bird.name: wicker basket (Bird)
wicker_basket_bird.purpose: A basket with a bird inside
wicker_basket_bird.description: A wicker basket carrying a Bird. Equip and left-click to release.

wicker_basket_owl.name: wicker basket (Owl)
wicker_basket_owl.purpose: A basket with an owl inside
wicker_basket_owl.description: A wicker basket carrying an Owl. Equip and left-click to release.

wooden_cage_cow.name: wooden cage (Cow)
wooden_cage_cow.purpose: A cage with a cow inside
wooden_cage_cow.description: A wooden cage carrying a Cow. Equip and left-click to release.

wooden_cage_sheep.name: wooden cage (Sheep)
wooden_cage_sheep.purpose: A cage with a sheep inside
wooden_cage_sheep.description: A wooden cage carrying a Sheep. Equip and left-click to release.

wooden_cage_deer.name: wooden cage (Deer)
wooden_cage_deer.purpose: A cage with a deer inside
wooden_cage_deer.description: A wooden cage carrying a Deer. Equip and left-click to release.

wooden_cage_redfox.name: wooden cage (Red Fox)
wooden_cage_redfox.purpose: A cage with a red fox inside
wooden_cage_redfox.description: A wooden cage carrying a Red Fox. Equip and left-click to release.

rope_lasso.name: rope lasso
rope_lasso.purpose: Loop over a passive animal to capture it. Equip + LMB within 15 studs.
rope_lasso.description: A weighted rawhide loop on a braided rope. Equip, walk within 15 studs of a passive animal, and left-click. The animal is packed into a basket or cage which goes directly to your inventory. Single-use: consumed on a successful catch.

wolf_lure.name: wolf lure
wolf_lure.purpose: Scent-bait for wolves. Equip + LMB to stake it.
wolf_lure.description: A scrap of cooked meat tied to a rope stake. Stake it in the ground and step back -- a wolf follows the blood-scent within 20-35 seconds. The lure is consumed on placement.

bear_lure.name: bear lure
bear_lure.purpose: Berry-scent bait for bears. Equip + LMB to stake it.
bear_lure.description: A cloth pouch of mashed berries and sap hung from a stake. Bears home in on the sweet smell within 25-40 seconds. The lure is consumed on placement.

flax_seed.name: flax seed
flax_seed.description: Small oily flax seeds. Sow them in tilled soil; the mature stalks ret down into strong line fibre.

turnip_seed.name: turnip seed
turnip_seed.description: Tiny turnip seeds. Fast, hardy, and forgiving -- the staple first crop on poor soil.

carrot_seed.name: carrot seed
carrot_seed.description: Saved seed from a bolted carrot. Slower than turnip but keeps the larder varied.

flax.name: flax stalks
flax.description: A bundle of pulled flax stalks. Ret them in water and beat out the bast -- the strongest plant fibre you can grow.

turnip.name: turnip
turnip.description: A firm, grown turnip pulled from tilled soil. Heavier and more filling than anything you forage wild.

ore.name: ore rock
ore.description: Mixed ore-bearing rock used as a generic early ore feed.

ore_copper_rock.name: copper ore rock
ore_copper_rock.description: Rocky ore with visible copper mineralization for early smelting or crushing.

ore_iron_rock.name: iron ore rock
ore_iron_rock.description: Dense iron-rich ore rock used in early crushing and bloomery feeds.

ore_tin_rock.name: tin ore rock
ore_tin_rock.description: Rocky tin ore for early alloying routes and small-scale smelting.

ore_iron_concentrate.name: iron ore concentrate
ore_iron_concentrate.description: Crushed and concentrated iron ore suitable for bloomery or blast-furnace feeds.

raw_ore_halite.name: raw halite ore
raw_ore_halite.description: Crystalline halite (rock salt) mined as raw ore; often processed by crushing and dissolution.

raw_ore_gypsum.name: raw gypsum ore
raw_ore_gypsum.description: Gypsum (calcium sulfate) mined as raw ore; used for plaster and industrial chemistry routes.

raw_ore_sylvite.name: raw sylvite ore
raw_ore_sylvite.description: Sylvite (potash ore) mined as raw ore; commonly processed into potassium salts.

raw_ore_chalcopyrite.name: raw chalcopyrite ore
raw_ore_chalcopyrite.description: Chalcopyrite (copper iron sulfide) mined as raw ore; typically upgraded into a sulfide concentrate.

raw_ore_galena.name: raw galena ore
raw_ore_galena.description: Galena (lead sulfide) mined as raw ore; commonly upgraded into a lead sulfide concentrate.

raw_ore_sphalerite.name: raw sphalerite ore
raw_ore_sphalerite.description: Sphalerite (zinc sulfide) mined as raw ore; commonly upgraded into a zinc sulfide concentrate.

raw_ore_pyrite.name: raw pyrite ore
raw_ore_pyrite.description: Pyrite (iron sulfide) mined as raw ore; can be used for sulfur chemistry or iron routes depending on tier.

raw_ore_hematite.name: raw hematite ore
raw_ore_hematite.description: Hematite (iron oxide) mined as raw ore; primary ironmaking feed in oxide routes.

raw_ore_magnetite.name: raw magnetite ore
raw_ore_magnetite.description: Magnetite (iron oxide) mined as raw ore; can be separated magnetically at higher tiers.

raw_ore_bauxite.name: raw bauxite ore
raw_ore_bauxite.description: Bauxite mined as raw ore; processed into alumina then aluminum at higher tiers.

raw_ore_limestone.name: raw limestone ore
raw_ore_limestone.description: Limestone (calcium carbonate rock) mined as raw ore; used for flux and lime chemistry.

raw_ore_silica.name: raw silica ore
raw_ore_silica.description: Quartz / silica mined as raw ore; used for glass, refractories, and later silicon routes.

raw_ore_flint.name: raw flint
raw_ore_flint.description: Flint (chert) mined as raw ore; early toolmaking feed and abrasive/mineral utility later.

raw_ore_obsidian.name: raw obsidian
raw_ore_obsidian.description: Obsidian (volcanic glass) mined as raw ore; sharp tool feed and specialty material later.

raw_ore_native_copper.name: raw native copper
raw_ore_native_copper.description: Native copper mined as raw ore; can be upgraded by crushing/sorting and refined into copper metal.

raw_ore_bog_iron.name: raw bog iron
raw_ore_bog_iron.description: Bog iron (limonite-rich iron ore) mined as raw ore; early iron route feedstock.

raw_ore_cassiterite.name: raw cassiterite ore
raw_ore_cassiterite.description: Cassiterite (tin oxide) found as heavy dark pebbles in stream gravels; the primary tin ore since antiquity.

raw_ore_spodumene.name: raw spodumene ore
raw_ore_spodumene.description: Spodumene (lithium ore) mined as raw ore; processed into lithium chemicals at higher tiers.

raw_ore_ree.name: raw rare earth ore
raw_ore_ree.description: Rare earth ore (e.g. bastnäsite) mined as raw ore; typically upgraded into a concentrate for chemical separation.

raw_ore_uraninite.name: raw uraninite ore
raw_ore_uraninite.description: Uraninite (pitchblende) mined as raw ore; processed via chemical routes at high tiers.

raw_ore_ilmenite.name: raw ilmenite ore
raw_ore_ilmenite.description: Ilmenite (iron titanium oxide) mined as raw ore; primary titanium ore found as dense black grains in placer sands. Chlorinated to TiCl4 then Kroll-reduced to titanium sponge at Space tier.

raw_ore_pentlandite.name: raw pentlandite ore
raw_ore_pentlandite.description: Pentlandite (nickel-iron sulfide) from magmatic sulfide deposits; primary nickel ore. Upgraded by flotation to Ni concentrate then refined via Mond carbonyl or electrowinning at Electric tier.

raw_ore_chromite.name: raw chromite ore
raw_ore_chromite.description: Chromite (iron chromium oxide) from ophiolite belts and layered igneous intrusions; sole commercial chromium ore. Arc-furnace smelted into ferrochrome at Electric tier; essential for stainless steel and superalloys.

raw_ore_fluorite.name: raw fluorite ore
raw_ore_fluorite.description: Fluorite (calcium fluoride) from hydrothermal veins; reacted with sulfuric acid to produce hydrofluoric acid, which enables aluminum smelting (cryolite synthesis) and uranium conversion (UF6 route). Minable from the start; HF production unlocked at Electric tier.

raw_ore_wolframite.name: raw wolframite ore
raw_ore_wolframite.description: Wolframite (iron-manganese tungstate) from high-temperature greisen and skarn deposits; converted via the APT route (NaOH digest -> ammonium paratungstate -> WO3) then hydrogen-reduced to tungsten powder for refractory nozzle throats and heater filaments.

raw_ore_columbite.name: raw columbite ore
raw_ore_columbite.description: Columbite (iron-manganese niobate) from rare-metal pegmatites; dissolved in HF then solvent-extracted to Nb2O5; aluminothermically reduced to niobium metal for superalloy additions and ferroniobium HSLA steel grades.

raw_ore_cobaltite.name: raw cobaltite ore
raw_ore_cobaltite.description: Cobaltite (CoAsS) from high-temperature hydrothermal veins; cobalt is extracted as a byproduct during Ni or Cu refining then electrodeposited. Used in superalloy compositions and samarium-cobalt magnets.

raw_ore_zircon.name: raw zircon ore
raw_ore_zircon.description: Zircon (ZrSiO4) recovered from beach and river placer sands alongside ilmenite; chlorinated then Kroll-reduced to zirconium sponge; alloyed into zircaloy for nuclear fuel cladding and high-temperature structural use.

concentrate_chalcopyrite.name: chalcopyrite concentrate
concentrate_chalcopyrite.description: Upgraded chalcopyrite fraction after milling + separation; higher copper mineral content and lower gangue. 2026-05-29 audit fix: was source-hinted but had no actual recipe; this is the gate to refined copper (bus_bar, knife switch, copper_wire). Stamp + screen at the stamp mill -- 3 raw -> 2 concentrate.

concentrate_galena.name: galena concentrate
concentrate_galena.description: Upgraded galena fraction after milling + separation; higher lead mineral content and lower gangue.

concentrate_sphalerite.name: sphalerite concentrate
concentrate_sphalerite.description: Upgraded sphalerite fraction after milling + separation; higher zinc mineral content and lower gangue.

concentrate_pyrite.name: pyrite concentrate
concentrate_pyrite.description: Upgraded pyrite fraction after milling + separation; used for sulfur chemistry routes at higher tiers.

concentrate_ree.name: rare earth concentrate
concentrate_ree.description: Upgraded rare earth mineral fraction after milling + separation; used for downstream chemical separation.

intermediate_alumina.name: alumina
intermediate_alumina.description: Aluminum oxide produced from bauxite; feedstock for aluminum smelting.

intermediate_sulfuric_acid.name: sulfuric acid
intermediate_sulfuric_acid.description: Strong acid used in leaching and industrial chemical processing routes.

intermediate_quicklime.name: quicklime
intermediate_quicklime.description: Reactive lime produced by calcining limestone; used as flux and in multiple chemical routes.

metal_zinc_ingot.name: zinc ingot
metal_zinc_ingot.description: Refined zinc ingot ready for alloying (brass) or industrial use.

metal_aluminum_ingot.name: aluminum ingot
metal_aluminum_ingot.description: Refined aluminum ingot produced from alumina at high tiers.

metal_aluminum_sheet.name: aluminum sheet
metal_aluminum_sheet.description: Aluminum rolled into thin structural sheet for skins, tanks and lightweight panels.

alloy_aluminum_aerospace.name: aerospace aluminum alloy
alloy_aluminum_aerospace.description: Copper-bearing aerospace aluminum alloy (2000-series-like) used for airframes and pressure tanks.

metal_lead_ingot.name: lead ingot
metal_lead_ingot.description: Refined lead ingot ready for batteries, shielding, and alloys.

metal_iron_ingot.name: iron ingot
metal_iron_ingot.description: Refined iron product used as feedstock for steelmaking and iron parts.

waste_tailings.name: tailings
waste_tailings.description: Residual sand/mud and low-value material left after separation; may be reprocessed later at higher tiers.

waste_red_mud.name: red mud
waste_red_mud.description: Highly alkaline residue from alumina refining (bauxite processing); handled as industrial waste.

iron_ore_crushed.name: crushed iron ore
iron_ore_crushed.description: Roasted iron ore broken to walnut-fist size so it packs right and reduces evenly in the bloomery shaft.

wrought_iron_billet.name: wrought iron billet
wrought_iron_billet.description: A hammered bloom with most of its slag driven out -- dense but still rough and tapered. Drawn out next into measurable bar stock.

bloomery_slag.name: bloomery slag
bloomery_slag.description: Heavy glassy slag tapped from a bloomery -- silica gangue bound with iron oxide and lime. Piles up fast; usable as road fill, wall aggregate, or partial flux on the next smelt.

hammer_scale.name: hammer scale
hammer_scale.description: Dark flaky iron oxide thrown off hot iron under the hammer. Collectible as red-brown pigment, a mortar additive, or a cheap iron-rich feed.

wood_ash.name: wood ash
wood_ash.description: Fine grey ash left from a charcoal burn. Leached for lye (soap), saved as potash (glass flux, crop amendment).

bronze_axe_head.name: bronze axe head
bronze_axe_head.description: A bronze axe head poured in a stone mold and ground to an edge; lashes to a haft to make a bronze axe.

bronze_pick_head.name: bronze pick head
bronze_pick_head.description: A bronze pick head with two ground points, poured in a stone mold; hafts into a bronze pickaxe for oxide and sulfide ores.

bronze_shovel_blade.name: bronze shovel blade
bronze_shovel_blade.description: A dished bronze blade poured in a stone mold; hafts into a bronze shovel that moves more soil per swing than wood or bone.

wood_haft.name: wood haft
wood_haft.description: A straightened, smoothed length of hardwood shaped to seat a tool head. The shared handle for axes, picks, hammers, hoes, and adzes.

stone_axe_head.name: stone axe head
stone_axe_head.description: A fist-sized stone core knapped to a wedge and ground to an edge; lashes to a haft to make an axe.

stone_flakes.name: stone flakes
stone_flakes.description: Sharp-edged spalls struck off while knapping. Usable straight away as cutting edges, or hafted into a flake knife.

stone_adze_head.name: stone adze head
stone_adze_head.description: A stone core knapped and ground to a gouging edge set across the haft; lashes on to make an adze for hollowing and surfacing timber.

stone_hoe_blade.name: stone hoe blade
stone_hoe_blade.description: A flattened, edge-ground stone blade lashed broad-side to a haft to chop and turn soil.

stripped_log.name: stripped log
stripped_log.description: A log with the bark cleanly stripped off. Worked timber: easier to split into hafts and sticks than fighting a raw log.

adze.name: adze
adze.description: Hand tool for smoothing beams and shaping timbers.

animal_glue.name: animal glue
animal_glue.description: Protein-based adhesive for wood joins and repairs.

basket_coiled.name: basket (coiled)
basket_coiled.description: Light container for carrying fibers, sand, or ore fines.

bellows.name: bellows
bellows.description: Manually-operated air pump to raise fire temperature.

block_and_tackle.name: block-and-tackle
block_and_tackle.description: Rope and pulleys to reduce lifting effort.

bow_drill.name: bow drill
bow_drill.description: Cord-wrapped spindle for drilling or fire starting.

bow_saw_frame.name: bow saw frame
bow_saw_frame.description: Tensioned frame saw for cutting wood.

cam_wooden.name: cam (wooden)
cam_wooden.description: Wooden cam used to raise a hammer or actuate a tappet.

capstan.name: capstan
capstan.description: Vertical-axis winch for hauling lines and lifting lines.

carving_bench.name: carving bench
carving_bench.description: Bench with holdfasts used to craft tools and components. 2026-05-29: no dedicated model yet -- placeModel routes to the existing workbench so placement doesn't error out. When a real bench builder lands, drop the placeModel override.

charcoal_drum_kiln.name: charcoal drum kiln
charcoal_drum_kiln.description: Metal drum kiln with vents for better yield and cleaner burn.

charcoal_retort_kiln.name: charcoal retort kiln
charcoal_retort_kiln.description: Retort kiln that recovers volatiles; highest yield and cleanest.

chisel.name: chisel
chisel.description: Edge tool for carving joinery and sculpting stone.

cobblestone.name: cobblestone
cobblestone.description: Broken or rounded rock fragments used for crude walls, paths, and foundations.

clay_tuyere.name: clay tuyere
clay_tuyere.description: Fired-clay nozzle directing bellows air into the hearth/kiln.

coarse_screen.name: coarse screen
coarse_screen.description: Wooden frame with coarse mesh for initial sorting.

counterweight.name: counterweight
counterweight.description: Mass used to balance or power a drop mechanism.

drawknife.name: drawknife
drawknife.description: Pull-stroke blade for rapidly shaping or debarking wood.

potters_wheel_kick.name: potter's wheel (kick)
potters_wheel_kick.purpose: Form clay vessels quickly and evenly
potters_wheel_kick.description: Foot-driven turntable that lets a potter throw symmetric pots much faster than hand-coiling.

fire_pit.name: fire pit
fire_pit.description: Open hearth for cooking and small heating tasks.

flywheel.name: flywheel
flywheel.description: A heavy wheel that banks momentum -- it smooths uneven power and keeps things turning through a brief dip.

gold_pan.name: gold pan
gold_pan.description: Shallow pan for gravity separation in streams.

grease_tallow.name: grease (tallow)
grease_tallow.description: Rendered fat used to reduce friction on wooden bearings.

grog_crushed_ceramic_temper.name: grog (crushed ceramic temper)
grog_crushed_ceramic_temper.description: Crushed fired ceramic added to clay to reduce shrink/crack.

hemp_fiber.name: hemp fiber
hemp_fiber.description: Strong plant fiber for rope and textiles.

holdfast.name: holdfast
holdfast.description: Wedgeable clamp for securing work on a bench.

hoe.name: hoe
hoe.description: Basic agricultural tool for soil shaping and resource gathering.

kiln_vent_baffle.name: kiln vent baffle
kiln_vent_baffle.description: Movable baffle for adjusting kiln draft and temperature.

lever_pry_bar.name: lever (pry bar)
lever_pry_bar.description: Stout wooden lever for prying and moving heavy items.

lever_press.name: lever press
lever_press.description: Simple lever-based press for oils or squeezing materials.

mallet.name: mallet
mallet.description: Wooden mallet for assembly and shaping tasks.

pitch.name: pitch
pitch.description: Sticky resinous material for sealing seams and baskets. Reduced from tree resin in a small clay kiln; the only T0-era sealant that holds up under continuous water contact. 2026-05-29 audit fix: was referenced by carbon_filament_preform (Ch5 lamp chain) with no source; added inline recipe.

plumb_bob.name: plumb bob
plumb_bob.description: Weight on string for establishing the vertical.

plain_bearing_wood.name: plain bearing (wood)
plain_bearing_wood.description: Wooden bearing surface lubricated with tallow.

post_mill_windmill.name: post mill (windmill)
post_mill_windmill.description: Early windmill type; entire body rotates to face wind.

pulley.name: pulley
pulley.description: Wheel with a groove to guide rope; used in tackles.

quern_rotary.name: quern (rotary)
quern_rotary.description: Rotary hand mill with handle and hopper.

riffle_insert_sluice.name: riffle insert (sluice)
riffle_insert_sluice.description: Removable riffle insert to trap heavy particles in sluices.

rocker_box.name: rocker box
rocker_box.description: Cradle-like separator rocked by hand to concentrate ore.

rope_walk_jig.name: rope-walk jig
rope_walk_jig.description: Stretching jig to twist fibers into rope.

sail_cloth.name: sail cloth
sail_cloth.description: Textile cloth used for windmill sails.

screw_jack_wooden.name: screw jack (wooden)
screw_jack_wooden.description: Wooden screw mechanism for jacking or pressing.

sheave.name: sheave
sheave.description: Wheel component for blocks and tackles.

sieve.name: sieve
sieve.description: Fine mesh frame for sifting fines from coarse material.

sluice_box.name: sluice box
sluice_box.description: Inclined trough with riffles for gravity separation.

sluice_trough.name: sluice trough
sluice_trough.description: Trough sections to carry water to the sluice.

chute_wood.name: wooden chute
chute_wood.description: A short wooden trough bolted between two adjacent machines so items dropped at the high end slide to the low end. Gravity-fed: place with the input end higher than the output (LogisticsService routes items along the chute's facing direction).

spindle_drill.name: spindle drill
spindle_drill.description: Simple spindle drill for wood and soft materials.

stamp_mill_manual_trip_hammer.name: stamp mill (manual trip hammer)
stamp_mill_manual_trip_hammer.description: Cam-lifted hammer that drops to crush ore.

tailings_box.name: tailings box
tailings_box.description: Box placed at sluice outlet to capture tailings.

tailpole_windmill_yaw.name: tailpole (windmill yaw)
tailpole_windmill_yaw.description: Lever used to rotate a post mill into the wind.

tar.name: tar
tar.description: Viscous byproduct of charcoal making; useful as sealant.

temper_sand_or_grog.name: temper (sand or grog)
temper_sand_or_grog.description: Aggregate added to clay to reduce shrinkage and cracking.

tappet.name: tappet
tappet.description: Follower piece actuated by a cam to transfer motion.

tinder.name: tinder
tinder.description: Very dry easily ignited material for kindling fires.

treadle.name: treadle
treadle.description: Pedal mechanism that provides rotary motion to devices.

water_level.name: water level
water_level.description: Transparent tube level for establishing level across distance.

waterwheel_overshot.name: waterwheel (overshot)
waterwheel_overshot.description: Turns flowing water into rotational power. Site it where the water runs.

waterwheel_undershot.name: waterwheel (undershot)
waterwheel_undershot.description: Wheel driven by water flowing beneath; lower efficiency, easy siting.

wedge.name: wedge
wedge.description: Simple wedge for splitting wood and cracking rocks.

wheel_and_axle.name: wheel-and-axle
wheel_and_axle.description: Basic rotating element to transmit force and motion.

windlass.name: windlass
windlass.description: Horizontal drum winch for lifting and pulling.

wooden_dowel.name: wooden dowel
wooden_dowel.description: Cylindrical wooden pin for joinery and peg gears.

wooden_gear_peg_toothed.name: wooden gear (peg-toothed)
wooden_gear_peg_toothed.description: Wooden gear with pegs used for low-speed drives.

wooden_peg.name: wooden peg
wooden_peg.description: Tapered peg for fastening wooden assemblies.

wooden_tub.name: wooden tub
wooden_tub.description: Large open container for washing or soaking materials.

timber_beam_rough.name: rough timber beam
timber_beam_rough.description: Hewn timber beam squared with axe and adze; used for posts, sills, and lifting frames.

board_plank_rough.name: rough board plank
board_plank_rough.description: Split or roughly sawn planks used for screens, troughs, boxes, and simple housings.

shaft_wood_round.name: wooden shaft (round)
shaft_wood_round.description: Turned or shaved wooden shaft used for wheels, windlasses, and early line shafts.

tool_stone_knife_hafted.name: hafted stone knife
tool_stone_knife_hafted.description: Stone knife hafted to a wooden handle for safer, stronger cutting.

item_questbook.name: quest book
item_questbook.description: A worn field journal tracking your progress through the ages. Right-click to open the Quest Log at any time. You can also press the backtick (`) key.

lootbag_curated_first_camp.name: First Camp Gift
lootbag_curated_first_camp.description: A small pack of essentials to get your first real camp going -- right-click to open it.

lootbag_tier_I.name: Survival Satchel
lootbag_tier_I.description: A bag of Tier I resources -- right-click to open it.

lootbag_tier_II.name: Pioneer's Pouch
lootbag_tier_II.description: A bag of Tier II resources -- right-click to open it.

lootbag_tier_III.name: Engineer's Kit
lootbag_tier_III.description: A bag of Tier III resources -- right-click to open it.

lootbag_tier_IV.name: Steelworker's Crate
lootbag_tier_IV.description: A crate of Tier IV (steel-age) resources -- right-click to open it.

lootbag_tier_V.name: Electrician's Locker
lootbag_tier_V.description: A locker of Tier V (electric-age) resources -- right-click to open it.

lootbag_tier_VI.name: Solid-State Cache
lootbag_tier_VI.description: A cache of Tier VI (solid-state) resources -- right-click to open it.

lootbag_tier_VII.name: Orbital Crate
lootbag_tier_VII.description: A crate of Tier VII (space-age) resources -- right-click to open it.

lootbag_curated_iron_age.name: Iron Age Head Start
lootbag_curated_iron_age.description: A curated selection of iron-age materials to get your metallurgy moving -- right-click to open it.

forest_twigs_fine.name: fine forest twigs
forest_twigs_fine.description: Handful of matchstick-thin forest twigs that catch quickly and help build a first flame.

forest_stick_scatter.name: forest stick scatter
forest_stick_scatter.description: Loose scatter of small forest sticks gathered from the ground; useful as quick starter fuel or light framework pieces.

stone_river_rounded.name: rounded river stone
stone_river_rounded.description: Water-rounded cobbles from stream beds used for hammerstones, hearth rings, or anchors.

plant_fibers.name: plant fibers
plant_fibers.description: Pulled plant fibers suitable for twisting into basic cordage and lashings.

rope.name: rope
rope.description: Simple twisted cord made from plant fibers; used for lashings, ties, and basic tool binding.

tool_stone_flake.name: stone flake
tool_stone_flake.description: Chipped stone flake used as a disposable cutting or scraping edge.

camp_marker_basic.name: camp marker (simple)
camp_marker_basic.description: Tripod of branches with a visible tag; marks the camp’s working area and acts as the anchor for early progression. Hand-craft: 1 branch bundle + 2 rounded river stones + 1 rope (2×2 grid).

scarecrow_basic.name: scarecrow (rag-and-stick)
scarecrow_basic.description: A rag-draped stick figure on a stake. Animals don't recognise it as a threat but won't approach crops in its shadow either. Effective for about 20 studs around the post.

forest_bark_strip.name: curled bark strip
forest_bark_strip.purpose: Crude bark tie and cover strip
forest_bark_strip.description: Curled strip of bark; can be split into rough ties or used as small cover pieces.

forest_bark_slabs.name: bark slabs
forest_bark_slabs.description: Hand-sized bark slabs pried or gathered from fallen logs; useful as quick shingle pieces or container panels.

bark_bucket.name: bark bucket
bark_bucket.purpose: Carry water short-distance. Equip + RMB at water to fill.
bark_bucket.description: Folded bark vessel lashed with cordage; works for short trips and quick camp water, not long-term storage. To fill: equip the bucket, aim at water (river, pond, surf), and press right mouse button -- the bucket is consumed and you receive a filled bark_bucket_water.

water_raw.name: raw water
water_raw.description: Raw surface water collected from streams/ponds; used as an input for filling containers. Not guaranteed safe.

bark_bucket_water.name: water-filled bark bucket
bark_bucket_water.description: Bark bucket filled with surface water. Heavy, sloshes, and not meant to be stored long-term. Equip the bark bucket, aim at a river or pond, and press RMB to fill it.

bark_bucket_water_treated.name: treated water (bark bucket)
bark_bucket_water_treated.description: Bark bucket of treated water (boiled). Safer to drink for a limited time if kept covered. Boil bark_bucket_water at a campfire (t0_boil_bark_bucket).

tinder_dry_fibers.name: dry fiber tinder
tinder_dry_fibers.description: Fluffed inner bark or dry grass fibers that take an ember quickly for fire starting.

stick_kindling_dry.name: dry kindling sticks
stick_kindling_dry.description: Finger-thick dry sticks used after tinder catches to build a stable fire.

stick_dry_small.name: small dry sticks
stick_dry_small.description: Short dry twigs from dead branches; ideal for light framework, skewers, and small feeding fuel.

fire_drill_kit.name: fire drill kit
fire_drill_kit.purpose: Equip + LMB near campfire to ignite (needs tinder)
fire_drill_kit.description: Friction-fire kit of hearth board and spindle. To use: 1) place a campfire_survival station, 2) equip this kit, 3) walk within 6 studs of the campfire, 4) press LMB to use the tool. The kit spins the spindle, consumes 1 tinder_dry_fibers from your inventory, and lights the campfire. The kit itself has limited durability and wears down with use.

fire_ring_stones.name: stone fire ring
fire_ring_stones.description: Ring of stones that contains coals and protects nearby ground from flame. Built at the camp marker: 6 rounded river stones (t0_build_fire_ring).

campfire_survival.name: survival campfire
campfire_survival.description: Simple open campfire for cooking and warmth. Built inside a fire ring for basic control.

drying_rack_survival.name: drying rack (survival)
drying_rack_survival.description: Simple rack for hanging strips of meat, sinew, or hides in moving air.

butchering_block_basic.name: butchering block (basic)
butchering_block_basic.description: Heavy log block bedded on stones; gives a stable surface for cutting and breakdown work.

foraged_berries.name: foraged berries
foraged_berries.description: Freshly picked wild berries still needing identification. Combine two handfuls to pick out the safe ones.

food_berries_safe.name: safe berries
food_berries_safe.description: Handful of wild berries identified as safe to eat; spoil quickly but give fast energy.

food_nuts_safe.name: safe nuts
food_nuts_safe.description: Shelled wild nuts selected as safe to eat; slower-burning, high-energy food.

food_cooked_simple.name: simple cooked food
food_cooked_simple.description: Generic early cooked food--roasted nuts, warmed berries, or other simple campfire prep--safer and more filling than eating cold and raw.

thatch_bundle.name: bundle of thatch
thatch_bundle.description: Bundle of grasses/reeds for thatching roofs or stuffing bedding.

bedroll_straw.name: straw bedroll
bedroll_straw.description: Roll of thatch and bark ties; basic insulation between you and the ground.

cloak_ragged.name: ragged cloak
cloak_ragged.description: Rough cloak stitched from scraps and cordage; low but useful insulation.

hood_ragged.name: ragged hood
hood_ragged.description: Simple hood to cut heat loss from the head and neck.

wraps_ragged.name: ragged leg wraps
wraps_ragged.description: Strips of fabric or hide wrapped around the legs to cut wind and wet exposure.

footwraps_ragged.name: ragged footwraps
footwraps_ragged.description: Crude footwraps to reduce ground chill and keep feet warmer.

hammerstone.name: hammerstone
hammerstone.description: A sturdy rounded cobble selected for use as a striking stone; useful as-is or hafted into a tool.

stone.name: fieldstone
stone.description: Rough fieldstone picked from the soil surface or dug from shallow pits; used in construction and tool-making.

forest_twigs_mixed.name: mixed forest twigs
forest_twigs_mixed.purpose: Mixed twigs from the forest floor
forest_twigs_mixed.description: A handful of mixed-size twigs collected from general forest litter; useful for kindling and small framework.

log_small_dry.name: small dry log
log_small_dry.description: Small-diameter fully dried log cut or broken from deadfall; good for fires and camp construction.

log_medium_dry.name: medium dry log
log_medium_dry.description: Thicker dead trunk section cut to camp length; burns long and works as structural timber.

log_oak.name: fresh oak log
log_oak.description: Short green oak log from a felled tree; dense and heavy. Burns poorly until dried.

log_birch.name: fresh birch log
log_birch.description: Light birch log from a felled tree; excellent bark for fire-starting even while green.

log_pine.name: fresh pine log
log_pine.description: Resinous pine log; sticky with sap and burns hot and fast once dried.

log_maple.name: fresh maple log
log_maple.description: Dense maple log; slow to dry but burns steadily and produces good coals.

maple_sap.name: maple sap
maple_sap.description: Clear sweet sap bled from a living maple. Drink raw for a small calorie boost or reduce into syrup.

log_beech.name: fresh beech log
log_beech.description: Heavy beech log; one of the densest hardwoods. Long drying time but outstanding fuel value.

log_ash.name: fresh ash log
log_ash.description: Tough straight-grained ash log; prized for tool handles and burns well even slightly green.

log_hardwood.name: fresh hardwood log
log_hardwood.description: Log from an unidentified hardwood; serviceable fuel and construction timber once dried.

log_spruce.name: fresh spruce log
log_spruce.description: Resinous spruce log; lighter than oak, splits cleanly into boards and poles. Must be dried for optimal fuel.

log_larch.name: fresh larch log
log_larch.description: Durable larch log; the only conifer whose wood resists rot even fresh. Good for outdoor posts and skids.

log_fir.name: fresh fir log
log_fir.description: Dense fir log from alpine elevations; resin-rich and slow to decay. Preferred for structural rafters.

log_willow.name: fresh willow log
log_willow.description: Soft flexible willow; carves easily and weaves into baskets and hurdles when split into withies.

log_alder.name: fresh alder log
log_alder.description: Alder hardens in water instead of rotting; ideal for watermill paddles, boat ribs, and wet-ground posts.

log_oak_dry.name: dry oak log
log_oak_dry.description: Air-dried oak; dense fuel that burns hot and long with good coals.

log_birch_dry.name: dry birch log
log_birch_dry.description: Light dry birch; catches quickly and burns bright, good for starting fires fast.

log_pine_dry.name: dry pine log
log_pine_dry.description: Dry resinous pine; burns very hot and fast with visible flame, ideal for forge work.

log_maple_dry.name: dry maple log
log_maple_dry.description: Dense dry maple; steady clean burn with few sparks.

log_beech_dry.name: dry beech log
log_beech_dry.description: The densest dry hardwood available; burns the longest and hottest of all common species.

log_ash_dry.name: dry ash log
log_ash_dry.description: Dry ash; one of the few woods that burns acceptably even slightly green, and very well when dry.

log_hardwood_dry.name: dry hardwood log
log_hardwood_dry.description: Dried log from an unidentified hardwood species; reliable campfire fuel.

log_spruce_dry.name: dry spruce log
log_spruce_dry.description: Dry spruce burns hot and fast -- excellent kindling and quick fire-starting in alpine camps.

log_larch_dry.name: dry larch log
log_larch_dry.description: Dense dry larch; burns steadily with good heat output and low smoke.

log_fir_dry.name: dry fir log
log_fir_dry.description: High-resin fir; burns intensely and produces excellent coals for overnight fires.

log_willow_dry.name: dry willow log
log_willow_dry.description: Dry willow burns quickly and cleanly with little smoke; useful as campfire starter.

log_alder_dry.name: dry alder log
log_alder_dry.description: Dry alder gives a moderate steady flame and is traditionally used to smoke fish.

pine_resin.name: pine resin
pine_resin.description: Amber resin scraped or tapped from pine bark wounds; used as adhesive, torch fuel, and waterproofing.

spruce_resin.name: spruce resin
spruce_resin.description: Clear-amber resin from spruce wounds; waterproofs leather, seals bark seams, and fuels torches. Harder than pine resin when cold.

fir_cone.name: fir cone
fir_cone.description: Resin-soaked cone that catches fire easily; also used as a tinder cache weight for fire lays and as animal bait.

alder_cone.name: alder catkin cone
alder_cone.description: Tiny dried alder catkin clusters; used in natural dyes (red-brown tones), leather tanning, and as fish bait.

hazel_rod.name: hazel rod
hazel_rod.description: Flexible coppiced hazel shoot; ideal for wicker weaving, basket frames, and light structural binding.

juniper_berry.name: juniper berry
juniper_berry.description: Pungent resinous berry; edible in small amounts and used as spice, preservative, and in leather tanning.

rowan_berry.name: rowan berry
rowan_berry.description: Tart mountain berry; mildly toxic raw in large quantities but safe cooked. Used in preserves and as a calorie source in lean highland winters.

blueberry.name: blueberry
blueberry.description: Sweet woodland berry; safe raw, dries well for storage. Common in shaded forest patches.

blackberry.name: blackberry
blackberry.description: Tart-sweet bramble berry; high in calories and vitamins. Often clusters along forest edges and clearings.

hawthorn_berry.name: hawthorn berry
hawthorn_berry.description: Mealy red berry with a single pit; mild flavour but reliable calories. Hedgerow staple.

sea_buckthorn_berry.name: sea buckthorn berry
sea_buckthorn_berry.description: Sharp citrus-tart orange berry; very high in vitamin C, common on coastal and disturbed ground.

hazelnut.name: hazelnut
hazelnut.description: Oily, calorie-dense nut from hazel shrubs; the inner kernel is a survival staple. Crack with a stone, eat raw or roast.

branch_bundle.name: branch bundle
branch_bundle.description: Bundle of dead branches gathered from trees and shrubs; used for camp building and light construction.

wood.name: construction wood
wood.description: Generic construction wood harvested from felled trees; used in building structures and stations.

willow.name: willow wands
willow.description: Pliable willow wands cut from riverside shrubs; ideal for weaving, basketry, and wattle work.

reeds.name: cut reeds
reeds.description: Cut tall reeds from pond and stream margins; used for thatch, cordage, and woven panels.

dirt.name: loose dirt
dirt.description: Loose mineral dirt dug from shallow pits or exposed banks; used in earth construction and filling.

bandage_cloth.name: cloth bandage
bandage_cloth.description: Strip of clean cloth wrapped over a wound. Heals 18 HP on use. The right thing to reach for after a wolf bite.

salve_herbal.name: herbal salve
salve_herbal.description: Crushed leaves mixed into rendered fat; applied to scrapes and burns. Heals 12 HP on use; milder than a clean bandage but you can make it from the field.

packed_earth_block.name: packed earth block
packed_earth_block.description: Three handfuls of dirt pressed and packed into a sturdy block. Right-click with a shovel to place a sandstone-coloured voxel -- useful for low walls, dams, or levees around farms. Looks distinct from natural dirt so you can tell the human-built parts of your land at a glance.

trophy_bird_hunter.name: trophy: bird hunter
trophy_bird_hunter.description: A small carved post marking five clean bird kills. Decorative camp piece.

trophy_lumberjack.name: trophy: lumberjack
trophy_lumberjack.description: A stump-and-axe display marking a fully fledged lumberjack. Decorative camp piece.

trophy_miner.name: trophy: miner
trophy_miner.description: A small cairn of carved stones marking serious time spent with a shovel and pick.

trophy_first_iron.name: trophy: iron-smelt
trophy_first_iron.description: A small bronze plaque commemorating the first iron ingot pulled from your bloomery.

trophy_deerstalker.name: trophy: deerstalker
trophy_deerstalker.description: Mounted antlers on a polished slab. Earned by hunting ten deer.

trophy_master_farmer.name: trophy: master farmer
trophy_master_farmer.description: A carved wooden bushel commemorating a hundred successful harvests.

trophy_builder.name: trophy: builder
trophy_builder.description: A scale-model plaque marking the founding of a real workshop.

trophy_wolfbane.name: trophy: wolfbane
trophy_wolfbane.description: A weathered wolf-pelt drum stand. Earned the hard way.

trophy_wanderer.name: trophy: wanderer
trophy_wanderer.description: A weathered traveller's marker carved with worn footprints. Awarded after walking 5000 studs (a few hours of unhurried exploration).

clay.name: raw clay
clay.description: Sticky clay dug from riverbanks or subsoil; can be formed into pots and fired in a kiln.

hide.name: raw animal hide
hide.description: Fresh hide from a hunted animal before any tanning; can be dried into rawhide cord or cured into leather.

grain.name: wild grain
grain.description: Seed heads stripped from wild grasses; can be ground into meal or flour.

resin.name: tree resin
resin.description: Sticky pine or spruce resin collected from bark wounds; used as adhesive, waterproofing, and torch fuel. 2026-05-29 audit fix: pickable form is species-specific spruce_resin; this generic resin form is what every downstream recipe consumes, so the inline recipe converts the spruce form 1:1.

kindling.name: kindling bundle
kindling.description: Bundle of carefully selected dry, finger-thick sticks for bridging tinder to larger fuel.

mud.name: wet mud
mud.description: Scooped mud from puddles or stream banks; used for daub, sealing, and earthen construction.

sand.name: loose sand
sand.description: Loose sand from river bars or lake shores; used in construction, casting, and filtration.

stone_knife_flake.name: stone knife flake
stone_knife_flake.description: A large sharp flake struck from a river cobble; the primary blank for hafting into a stone knife.

tool_hammerstone_basic.name: hammerstone (hafted)
tool_hammerstone_basic.description: A rounded cobble wrapped with plant fibres for a secure hand grip; used for knapping and light percussion work.

stone_axe_ground.name: ground stone axe
stone_axe_ground.description: A stone head shaped and edge-ground then lashed to a branch handle; used for splitting wood and rough carpentry.

shovel.name: crude shovel
shovel.description: Simple digging implement for breaking and moving soil; used in pit-digging and earthworks.

tool_stone_pick_basic.name: stone pick (lashed)
tool_stone_pick_basic.purpose: Crude stone pick head lashed to a stick
tool_stone_pick_basic.description: A rough stone head bound to a dry stick with rope; barely holds but can crack surface rock and mine primitive ore nodes.

shovel_bone.name: bone shovel
shovel_bone.description: A large bone lashed flat to a stick; the earliest digging implement. Breaks after modest use.

rawhide_cord.name: rawhide cord
rawhide_cord.description: Hide strips cut and dried on a rack; shrinks into tough rawhide cord for lashing and binding.

charcoal.name: charcoal
charcoal.description: Charcoal produced by smouldering wood in a covered kiln; hot, clean-burning fuel for forges and ceramics.

clay_pot.name: clay pot (greenware)
clay_pot.description: Hand-formed clay pot before firing; fragile until kiln-fired into durable earthenware.

earthenware_vessel.name: earthenware vessel
earthenware_vessel.description: Kiln-fired earthenware pot suitable for storing water, grain, and preserves.

earthenware_brick.name: earthenware brick
earthenware_brick.description: Kiln-fired clay brick for construction of permanent hearths, kilns, and early masonry.

meal_ground_grain.name: ground grain meal
meal_ground_grain.description: Grain ground on a saddle quern into coarse meal; can be mixed with water and baked.

flour.name: flour
flour.description: Finely ground grain flour from extended milling; used for baking and dough.

debris_shelter_frame.name: debris shelter frame
debris_shelter_frame.description: Lashed branch-bundle A-frame ready for debris or thatch covering; the first step to sleeping sheltered.

debris_shelter_lean_to.name: debris lean-to shelter
debris_shelter_lean_to.description: Thatch-covered lean-to that significantly reduces heat loss and rain exposure while sleeping.

bedroll_basic.name: basic bedroll
bedroll_basic.description: Straw core wrapped in a hide cover and stitched with rawhide cord; significantly better than a bare straw roll.

waterskin_rawhide.name: rawhide waterskin
waterskin_rawhide.purpose: Portable water carry (rawhide). Equip + RMB at water to fill.
waterskin_rawhide.description: Folded and stitched raw hide waterskin; holds more water than a bark bucket and can be carried longer distances. To fill: equip the skin, aim at water, and press right mouse button -- the empty skin is consumed and you receive a filled waterskin_rawhide_filled.

waterskin_rawhide_filled.name: water-filled rawhide waterskin
waterskin_rawhide_filled.description: Rawhide waterskin filled with surface water; sloshy but carries a useful amount.

clay_pot_water.name: clay pot (water)
clay_pot_water.description: A fired earthenware pot filled with water; heavier but more durable than bark containers.

torch_resin_pine.name: pine resin torch
torch_resin_pine.description: Dry stick wrapped with plant fibre and soaked in pine resin; burns longer and steadier than bare kindling.

snare_simple.name: simple snare
snare_simple.description: Rawhide cord loop staked along game trails; set and leave to catch rabbits and small animals.

fishing_trap_weir.name: wicker fish trap
fishing_trap_weir.description: Woven willow funnel trap placed in a stream channel; fish swim in and cannot find the exit.

mortar_stone.name: stone mortar
mortar_stone.description: Pecked depression in a stone block; used with a pestle to grind grain, pigments, and bone meal.

pestle_stone.name: stone pestle
pestle_stone.description: Rounded stone fitted for pounding and grinding inside a stone mortar.

quern_saddle.name: saddle quern
quern_saddle.description: Smooth saddle-shaped grinding stone for sustained grain milling into meal and flour.

charcoal_clamp_earth_mound_kiln.name: earth-mound charcoal clamp
charcoal_clamp_earth_mound_kiln.description: Earth-covered wood mound that smoulders covered logs into charcoal over several hours.

earthen_kiln_updraft.name: earthen updraft kiln
earthen_kiln_updraft.description: Clay-and-stone updraft kiln for firing greenware into durable earthenware and bricks.

wattle_screen.name: wattle screen
wattle_screen.purpose: Woven panel for walls and windbreaks
wattle_screen.description: Willow-woven screen panel used for windbreaks, pit covers, and simple partitions.

pit_storage_cache.name: pit storage cache
pit_storage_cache.description: Wattle-lined underground cache with a covered entrance; keeps food cool and protected from scavengers.

storage_firewood_rack.name: firewood storage rack
storage_firewood_rack.description: Pole rack that keeps split firewood off wet ground so it dries more evenly.

storage_food_pit.name: food storage pit
storage_food_pit.description: Excavated and stone-lined pit for cool, insulated food storage between camp activities.

storage_crate_bark.name: bark storage crate
storage_crate_bark.description: A simple crate of lashed saplings with bark sides. Holds 27 item stacks -- the same as your pack. Keeps items between sessions.

storage_chest_wood.name: wooden chest
storage_chest_wood.description: A jointed and pegged wooden chest. Holds 27 item stacks. More durable and weather-resistant than bark.

waste_midden_basic.name: camp waste midden
waste_midden_basic.description: Shallow hollow ringed with branches marking the camp's designated waste deposit site.

workbench.name: workbench
workbench.purpose: Raised work surface for camp crafting
workbench.description: Flat log platform on legs providing a stable surface for detailed camp crafting work.

basalt.name: basalt
basalt.description: Dense dark basalt broken from volcanic outcrops; useful as heat-resistant hearth stone.

granite.name: granite
granite.description: Pale coarse granite from bedrock exposures; suitable for construction and rough grinding.

limestone.name: limestone
limestone.description: Pale limestone from cliff faces or eroded slopes; used as flux in metal smelting and for lime production.

limestone_rock.name: limestone nodule
limestone_rock.description: Individual nodule of limestone picked from talus or outcrops; same mineral as limestone slab but smaller. 2026-05-29 audit fix: mining limestone nodes yields `raw_ore_limestone`; this nodule form is what blast_furnace_small / bessemer recipes consume, so the inline recipe converts the raw form 1:1.

marble.name: marble
marble.description: Veined or banded marble from rare metamorphic outcrops; denser than limestone, used for decorative work.

rock_salt.name: rock salt
rock_salt.description: Crystalline halite chipped from dry lake beds or shallow cave deposits; direct food preservative.

sandstone.name: sandstone
sandstone.description: Sand-grain cemented sandstone from layered cliff faces; useful for rough grinding and mold carving.

slate.name: slate
slate.description: Thin-splitting slate from dark fissile beds; splits into flat plates useful as shingles and smooth surfaces.

water.name: water
water.description: Generic water resource used as a fluid system pointer. See water_raw for collectable surface water.

ice.name: ice block
ice.description: Block of solid ice cut from frozen ponds or packed snow; melts into water, useful for cold preservation.

snow.name: loose snow
snow.description: Loose snow scooped from open ground or drifts; melts into water and can be packed for insulation.

forest_leaf_scatter.name: scattered leaves
forest_leaf_scatter.purpose: Dry loose leaves from the forest floor
forest_leaf_scatter.description: A light handful of fallen leaves raked from the forest floor. Dry enough to use as tinder or pack into bedding.

forest_leaf_mat_packed.name: packed leaf mat
forest_leaf_mat_packed.description: A cohesive mat of leaves pressed together over time; useful for insulation and bedding.

forest_moss_tuft_small.name: small moss tuft
forest_moss_tuft_small.description: A small tuft of moss pulled from a damp rock or tree base; absorbs moisture and makes soft tinder when dried.

forest_moss_mat.name: moss mat
forest_moss_mat.purpose: Flat moss panel peeled from stone or log
forest_moss_mat.description: A cohesive mat of moss -- compact, slightly damp, useful as bedding or wound packing.

forest_needles_light.name: light conifer needles
forest_needles_light.purpose: Fresh surface layer of fallen needles
forest_needles_light.description: The lighter, fresher top layer of conifer needle fall. Burns quickly and catches easily when dry.

forest_needles_deep.name: deep conifer needles
forest_needles_deep.description: The older, darker needle layer compressed beneath the surface. Better for insulation and slow-burn tinder.

forest_cones_whole.name: whole cones
forest_cones_whole.description: Whole cones collected beneath conifer trees; burn steadily and can be used to start fires.

forest_cone_scales.name: cone scales
forest_cone_scales.description: Dry, papery scales from broken cones; light and catch fire easily.

forest_nut_shell_fragments.name: nut shell fragments
forest_nut_shell_fragments.purpose: Broken nut shells from the forest floor
forest_nut_shell_fragments.description: Fragments of hard nut shells scattered under feeding trees. Dense enough to hold an ember.

forest_humus_clod.name: humus clod
forest_humus_clod.description: A clod of dark, organic forest soil. Useful for early pottery clay mixing or as growing medium.

forest_fern_fronds_young.name: young fern fronds
forest_fern_fronds_young.description: Freshly unfurled fern fronds; pliable enough to weave or use as cover, and edible in small amounts.

forest_flower_heads_dry.name: dry flower heads
forest_flower_heads_dry.description: Dried flower heads shed from forest-edge plants; makes fine tinder and can be crushed for seed.

forest_branch_chunk_small.name: small branch chunk
forest_branch_chunk_small.purpose: Short segment of a fallen branch
forest_branch_chunk_small.description: A hand-length piece broken from a fallen branch. Useful for kindling or lashing.

forest_branch_chunk_rotten.name: rotten branch chunk
forest_branch_chunk_rotten.description: A crumbly piece of rotten wood. Burns poorly but can be dried and used to hold coals for fire-making.

forest_seed_mix_dry.name: dry forest seed mix
forest_seed_mix_dry.description: Dry mix of small seeds and husks gathered from the forest floor; useful for feed or early planting experiments.

log_small_green.name: small green log
log_small_green.description: Small log of freshly cut green wood; heavier and wetter than dry logs, ideal for carving or controlled drying.

meat_venison_raw.name: raw venison
meat_venison_raw.description: Raw venison; very lean and fine-grained. Cook before eating.

meat_venison_cooked.name: cooked venison
meat_venison_cooked.description: Lean venison roasted over the campfire; high-protein and clean-tasting.

meat_beef_raw.name: raw beef
meat_beef_raw.description: Raw beef from butchered cattle; rich in fat and protein, must be cooked.

meat_beef_cooked.name: cooked beef
meat_beef_cooked.description: Dense beef roasted over the campfire; filling and satisfying.

meat_mutton_raw.name: raw mutton
meat_mutton_raw.description: Raw mutton from a butchered sheep; fattier than venison. Cook before eating.

meat_mutton_cooked.name: cooked mutton
meat_mutton_cooked.description: Fatty mutton roasted over coals; high in calories with a strong flavour.

meat_pork_raw.name: raw pork
meat_pork_raw.description: Raw pork from wild boar; significantly fattier than domestic pork. Must be cooked thoroughly.

meat_pork_cooked.name: cooked pork
meat_pork_cooked.description: Wild boar pork cooked over a campfire; fatty, rich, and calorie-dense.

meat_poultry_raw.name: raw poultry
meat_poultry_raw.description: White meat from game birds and owls; must be cooked before eating.

meat_poultry_cooked.name: cooked poultry
meat_poultry_cooked.description: Game bird roasted on a stick; lean, mild white meat with little fat.

meat_rabbit_raw.name: raw rabbit meat
meat_rabbit_raw.description: Raw rabbit meat; one of the leanest wild meats. Too lean to subsist on alone without fat.

meat_rabbit_cooked.name: cooked rabbit
meat_rabbit_cooked.description: Rabbit meat roasted over the campfire; very lean and delicate.

meat_squirrel_raw.name: raw squirrel meat
meat_squirrel_raw.description: Very small amount of raw squirrel meat; barely enough for a snack.

meat_squirrel_cooked.name: cooked squirrel
meat_squirrel_cooked.description: Small portion of squirrel roasted on a stick; lean, mild, modest calories.

meat_raccoon_raw.name: raw raccoon meat
meat_raccoon_raw.description: Raw raccoon meat; darker and slightly gamey, edible once cooked properly.

meat_raccoon_cooked.name: cooked raccoon
meat_raccoon_cooked.description: Raccoon meat cooked over the campfire; dark, gamey, moderately fatty.

meat_fox_raw.name: raw fox meat
meat_fox_raw.description: Raw fox meat; lean and dark, with a distinctive wild flavour. Cook thoroughly.

meat_fox_cooked.name: cooked fox
meat_fox_cooked.description: Fox meat roasted over coals; lean and deeply flavoured.

meat_wolf_raw.name: raw wolf meat
meat_wolf_raw.description: Raw wolf meat; very tough and lean, strong wild flavour. Must be cooked.

meat_wolf_cooked.name: cooked wolf
meat_wolf_cooked.description: Wolf meat slow-roasted over the campfire; tough, lean, intensely flavoured.

meat_bear_raw.name: raw bear meat
meat_bear_raw.description: Raw black bear meat; very rich, dark red, heavily marbled with fat. Never eat raw.

meat_bear_cooked.name: cooked bear
meat_bear_cooked.description: Bear meat roasted thoroughly; enormously calorie-dense thanks to heavy fat marbling.

hide_fine_raw.name: fine raw hide
hide_fine_raw.description: Fresh hide from small game; can be cured into light leather.

hide_light_raw.name: light raw hide
hide_light_raw.description: Fresh hide from medium game; suitable for light leather goods after curing.

hide_thick_raw.name: thick raw hide
hide_thick_raw.description: Fresh heavy hide from large game; tough and durable once cured.

fat_raw.name: raw animal fat
fat_raw.description: Raw animal fat suitable for rendering into tallow or grease.

bone_large.name: large bone
bone_large.description: Large bone from big game; can be carved or used as a structural component.

bone_small.name: small bone
bone_small.description: Small bones useful for tools, needles, or cooking stock.

antler_pair.name: antler pair
antler_pair.description: Paired antlers harvested from large game; useful for tools and decoration.

tusk.name: tusk
tusk.description: Curved tusk from boar or similar; can be carved into tools or trophies.

fang.name: fang
fang.description: Sharp tooth from predators; useful for small tools or trophies.

claw.name: claw
claw.description: Claw from predators; useful for small tools or trophies.

feather_small.name: small feathers
feather_small.description: Small feathers from birds; useful for fletching or bedding.

pelt_fox.name: fox pelt
pelt_fox.description: Soft fox pelt suitable for warmth or trade once cured.

pelt_wolf.name: wolf pelt
pelt_wolf.description: Heavy wolf pelt; thick fur suitable for warm clothing after curing.

meat_camel_raw.name: raw camel meat
meat_camel_raw.description: Raw camel meat; dark, lean, and surprisingly tender. Cook before eating.

meat_vulture_raw.name: raw vulture meat
meat_vulture_raw.description: Stringy raw vulture meat; strongly gamey and tough, but sustaining once thoroughly cooked.

meat_lizard_raw.name: raw lizard meat
meat_lizard_raw.description: A small cut of raw lizard; lean and mild, quick to cook over a flame.

meat_seal_raw.name: raw seal meat
meat_seal_raw.description: Raw seal meat; very dark and oily, packed with the fat a body needs in the cold.

meat_turtle_raw.name: raw turtle meat
meat_turtle_raw.description: Raw sea turtle meat; firm and mild-flavoured, a hearty coastal meal once cooked.

crab_meat_raw.name: raw crab meat
crab_meat_raw.description: Pale raw meat picked from a crab; sweet and delicate once boiled or roasted.

shell_fragment.name: shell fragment
shell_fragment.description: A shard of crab carapace; sharp-edged and useful for small tools or grit.

chitin.name: chitin plate
chitin.description: A plate of hardened chitin from an arthropod; light and tough, useful for crafting.

venom_gland.name: venom gland
venom_gland.description: A scorpion's venom gland, removed whole; handle with care -- valuable for tinctures and coatings.

antler.name: antler
antler.description: A single shed or harvested antler; carve into tools, needles, or decoration.

feather_dark.name: dark feathers
feather_dark.description: Broad dark feathers from a vulture; coarse but good for fletching and bedding.

feather_white.name: white feathers
feather_white.description: Clean white feathers from a seabird; soft and well suited to fletching or insulation.

pelt_white.name: white fur pelt
pelt_white.description: Thick white pelt from an arctic fox; prized for warmth once cured.

cactus_fruit.name: cactus fruit
cactus_fruit.description: The ripe fruit of a desert cactus; jewel-bright, juicy, and a welcome source of water in the dunes.

coconut.name: coconut
coconut.description: A whole coconut gathered from a shoreline palm; crack it for sweet water and dense flesh.

deadwood.name: deadwood
deadwood.description: Sun-bleached dead wood broken from a snag; bone-dry and excellent firewood or rough timber.

cactus_water.name: cactus water
cactus_water.description: Cool water pressed from cactus fruit; a desert lifesaver that restores hydration fast.

coconut_flesh.name: coconut flesh
coconut_flesh.description: Rich white flesh cracked from a coconut; filling and calorie-dense.

venom_coating.name: venom coating
venom_coating.description: A sticky paste of scorpion venom and rendered fat; coat a blade or arrow to poison what it strikes.

arrow_fletching.name: arrow fletching
arrow_fletching.description: Feather vanes lashed with fibre, ready to fletch an arrow shaft for true flight.

down_insulation.name: feather down
down_insulation.description: A handful of soft seabird down; packed into clothing or bedding it traps warmth against the cold.

antler_needle.name: antler needle
antler_needle.description: A slender needle carved from antler; essential for stitching hide and pelts into clothing.

chitin_plating.name: chitin plating
chitin_plating.description: Hardened chitin plates lashed together with cord; a light, tough base for primitive armour.

shell_lime.name: burnt shell lime
shell_lime.description: Crab shell roasted to a white powder; a caustic lime used in tanning and early chemistry.

fur_white_cured.name: cured white fur
fur_white_cured.description: An arctic fox pelt scraped and dried into supple white fur; prime material for cold-weather gear.

meat_camel_cooked.name: grilled camel
meat_camel_cooked.description: Camel meat grilled over the fire; lean, dark, and richly satisfying after a desert trek.

meat_vulture_cooked.name: roasted vulture
meat_vulture_cooked.description: Tough vulture meat slow-roasted until palatable; strong-flavoured but it fills the belly.

meat_lizard_cooked.name: roasted lizard
meat_lizard_cooked.description: A skewer of lizard meat roasted over coals; lean, mild, and quick to prepare.

meat_seal_cooked.name: cooked seal
meat_seal_cooked.description: Seal meat cooked through; dark, oily, and enormously calorie-dense -- prized in the cold.

meat_turtle_cooked.name: cooked turtle
meat_turtle_cooked.description: Sea turtle meat cooked through; firm, mild, and hearty -- a feast on the shore.

crab_meat_cooked.name: cooked crab meat
crab_meat_cooked.description: Sweet crab meat cooked through over the coals; delicate and rich.

carcass_cow.name: cow carcass
carcass_cow.description: Whole cow carcass from a slaughtered animal; must be broken down at a butchering block.

carcass_sheep.name: sheep carcass
carcass_sheep.description: Whole sheep carcass; break down at the butchering block for meat, pelt, and fat.

carcass_deer.name: deer carcass
carcass_deer.description: Whole deer carcass; process at the butchering block for venison, hide, and antlers.

carcass_bird.name: game bird carcass
carcass_bird.description: Whole small game bird; pluck and eviscerate at the butchering block for meat and feathers.

carcass_rabbit.name: rabbit carcass
carcass_rabbit.description: Small rabbit carcass; skin and dress at the butchering block for meat, hide, and bones.

carcass_squirrel.name: squirrel carcass
carcass_squirrel.description: Tiny carcass; yields a little white meat and a small hide when processed.

carcass_raccoon.name: raccoon carcass
carcass_raccoon.description: Raccoon carcass; break down for red meat, light hide, and small bones.

carcass_owl.name: owl carcass
carcass_owl.description: Owl carcass; pluck for feathers then dress for a small amount of white meat.

carcass_fox.name: red fox carcass
carcass_fox.description: Fox carcass; pelt and dress at the butchering block for a quality fur pelt and lean meat.

carcass_wolf.name: wolf carcass
carcass_wolf.description: Large predator carcass; yields a heavy pelt, substantial red meat, and a fang.

carcass_bear.name: black bear carcass
carcass_bear.description: Massive carcass; rich in fat, red meat, and a thick pelt. Requires effort to break down.

carcass_boar.name: wild boar carcass
carcass_boar.description: Boar carcass; pork, fat, tusks and a thick hide come out of a proper breakdown.

meat_red_raw.name: raw red meat
meat_red_raw.description: Raw red meat from large herbivores; must be cooked or preserved quickly.

meat_white_raw.name: raw white meat
meat_white_raw.description: Raw white meat from small game or birds; leaner than red meat, cook before eating.

fat_raw_mixed.name: raw mixed animal fat
fat_raw_mixed.description: Mixed raw fat from butchered animals; render over a campfire to produce clean tallow.

fat_rendered_tallow.name: rendered tallow
fat_rendered_tallow.description: Slowly melted and strained animal fat; used for waterproofing, lighting, and leather conditioning.

bone_raw_large.name: large raw bone
bone_raw_large.description: Large bone from big game; can be cracked for marrow or ground into bone meal.

bone_raw_small.name: small raw bone
bone_raw_small.description: Small bones from carcass joints; useful for needles, cooking stock, or bone meal.

bone_meal_ground.name: ground bone meal
bone_meal_ground.description: Cracked and pounded bones ground into coarse bone meal; used for early farming or glue-making.

hide_cow_raw.name: raw cow hide
hide_cow_raw.description: Thick fresh hide from a cow; suitable for heavy tanned leather after bark-liquor soaking.

hide_deer_raw.name: raw deer hide
hide_deer_raw.description: Fresh deer hide; thinner and more supple than cow hide, cures into soft light leather.

pelt_sheep_raw.name: raw sheep pelt
pelt_sheep_raw.description: Fresh sheep pelt with fleece intact; can be prepared into warm fur with a tanned backing.

leather_heavy_tanned.name: heavy tanned leather
leather_heavy_tanned.description: Heavy leather tanned in bark liquor from cow hide; used for footwear soles, harness, and straps.

leather_light_tanned.name: light tanned leather
leather_light_tanned.description: Light leather from deer hide; softer and more pliable, suited to clothing, pouches, and lacing.

pelt_fur_prepared.name: prepared fur pelt
pelt_fur_prepared.description: Washed and worked pelt with the fur still on; the fleece stays soft while the hide cures flexible.

offal_edible_mixed.name: mixed edible offal
offal_edible_mixed.description: Assorted edible organs (liver, heart, kidney) from butchered animals; must be cooked promptly.

offal_inedible_mixed.name: inedible offal
offal_inedible_mixed.description: Inedible viscera and waste from butchery; useful as fertiliser for the midden.

blood_animal_fresh.name: fresh animal blood
blood_animal_fresh.description: Blood collected during butchery; must be dried quickly into blood meal or it spoils.

blood_meal_dried.name: dried blood meal
blood_meal_dried.description: Dried and powdered animal blood; high-nitrogen fertiliser for early crops.

giblets_bird_mixed.name: mixed bird giblets
giblets_bird_mixed.description: Mixed organ cuts from game birds; cook quickly or they spoil.

feather_bundle_large.name: large feather bundle
feather_bundle_large.description: Large primary and secondary feathers plucked from game birds; used for fletching and quill work.

feather_down_fill.name: down fill feathers
feather_down_fill.description: Soft fluffy down gathered from beneath bird contour feathers; excellent insulation for bedding and clothing.

wool_fleece_raw.name: raw wool fleece
wool_fleece_raw.description: Freshly sheared or peeled sheep fleece before washing and combing; used for spinning and felt-making.

sinew_bundle_dried.name: dried sinew bundle
sinew_bundle_dried.description: Leg sinew stripped from large game and dried into tough natural cordage for lashing and rope-making.

horn_cattle_raw.name: raw cattle horn
horn_cattle_raw.description: Solid cattle horn removed from the skull; can be hollowed into a drinking vessel or worked into tools.

antler_deer_raw.name: raw deer antler
antler_deer_raw.description: Deer antler from butchery or seasonal shed; hard and workable into tools, needles, and hafts.

hoof_keratin_chunk.name: hoof keratin chunk
hoof_keratin_chunk.description: Dense keratin from split hooves; can be boiled for glue stock or ground as hoof meal.

gelatin_animal_raw.name: raw animal gelatin
gelatin_animal_raw.purpose: Collagen stock from bones and hide
gelatin_animal_raw.description: Thick gelatinous stock simmered from small bones and hide offcuts; sets firm when cool.

cooked_meat_skewer.name: cooked meat skewer
cooked_meat_skewer.description: A chunk of meat cooked on a stick over the campfire; safe to eat and gives a decent calorie boost.

food_meat_cooked_simple.name: cooked meat (generic)
food_meat_cooked_simple.description: A simply cooked portion of meat with no specific recipe -- used as a fallback output when no skewer or vessel is available. Same calorie value as a skewer without the stick byproduct.

milk_raw.name: raw milk
milk_raw.description: Raw milk obtained by hand-milking a tame cow; drink soon or process before it spoils.

wool_raw.name: raw wool
wool_raw.description: Raw wool shorn or hand-pulled from a tame sheep; needs washing and carding before spinning.

acorn.name: acorn
acorn.description: Ripe acorns fallen from oak trees. Bitter raw but edible once leached; used as bait for deer and squirrels.

berry_bundle.name: berry bundle
berry_bundle.description: A mixed bundle of ripe forest berries -- hawthorn, elderberry, and bramble. Attractive to bear, fox, and boar.

carrot.name: wild carrot
carrot.description: A pale, knobby wild carrot dug from meadow soil. Horses and deer find it irresistible.

clover.name: clover bundle
clover.description: A bunch of red and white clover pulled from open ground. Preferred grazing material for rabbits and deer.

feed_bundle.name: forage feed bundle
feed_bundle.description: A rough bundle of dried grass, hay, and foraged herbs. General-purpose animal feed suitable for most herbivores.

feed_grain.name: feed grain
feed_grain.description: Loose threshed grain suitable for feeding chickens, horses, pigs, and cattle. Also usable as a taming lure.

fish_raw.name: raw fish
fish_raw.description: A whole raw fish pulled from a river or lake. Attractive bait for bears and otters; edible but best cooked.

fishing_rod_basic.name: basic fishing rod
fishing_rod_basic.purpose: Equip + RMB near water to fish
fishing_rod_basic.description: A simple cane rod strung with plant-fiber line and a bent-thorn hook. Equip it, walk to the water's edge, and press RMB to cast. A float bobber signals a bite -- press F quickly when it dips. Wears out over roughly 20 catches. Craft at a camp marker: 2 dry sticks + 3 plant fibers + 1 stone knife flake.

grass_bundle.name: grass bundle
grass_bundle.description: A bundle of fresh meadow grass. Basic grazing supplement for cattle, horses, and sheep.

grub.name: grub
grub.description: A fat grub or beetle larva dug from soil or rotting wood. High protein; bait for bears, badgers, and birds.

honeycomb.name: honeycomb
honeycomb.description: A waxy comb section dripping with raw honey. Irresistible to bears; also a calorie-dense human food.

mushroom.name: foraged mushroom
mushroom.description: A foraged mushroom from shaded forest floor. Edible varieties attract boar and deer; toxic varieties still work as bait.

nut_bundle.name: nut bundle
nut_bundle.description: Mixed forest nuts -- hazel, walnut, beech. Calorie-dense bait for squirrels, deer, and boar.

salt.name: salt lick
salt.description: A lump of rock salt placed as a lick. Draws deer, cattle, and horses from a distance; consumed slowly.

anvil_stone.name: stone anvil block
anvil_stone.description: A heavy flat-faced stone block set on a stable log base; used for cold-hammering native copper and as a casting support for early bronze work. Precedes the iron anvil by the entire Bronze Age.

roasting_pit.name: ore roasting pit
roasting_pit.purpose: Drive off moisture from ore before bloomery
roasting_pit.description: A shallow earthen pit lined with stone and charged with ore and charcoal; used to roast iron ores before bloomery reduction. Removes moisture and cracks ore structure for better yield.

clay_bloomery_furnace.name: clay bloomery furnace
clay_bloomery_furnace.description: Small shaft furnace built from clay and brick, fired with charcoal and fed bellows-blown air. Reaches the 1100-1200C needed to reduce iron ore to a spongy bloom.

iron_anvil_block.name: iron anvil block
iron_anvil_block.description: Solid wrought-iron block that provides a hard surface for hammering hot metal. Heavier than stone allows precision shaping of iron and steel.

forge_hearth.name: forge hearth
forge_hearth.description: Side-blown charcoal forge built from firebrick and clay with a clay tuyere and bellows. Used to reheat blooms and hammer them into wrought-iron bars and shaped tools.

copper_nugget_hammered.name: hammered copper nugget
copper_nugget_hammered.description: Native copper hammered into a workable nugget using a hammerstone. Work-hardens with each strike; anneal in fire to restore workability.

crucible_clay.name: clay crucible
crucible_clay.description: A small clay crucible shaped for holding metal charges in a kiln. Withstands copper-melting temperatures (~1085C).

stone_mold_ingot.name: stone ingot mold
stone_mold_ingot.description: A two-piece sandstone mold carved for pouring molten metal into ingot shape. Reusable; rinse and dry between pours.

copper_ingot.name: copper ingot
copper_ingot.description: Pure copper ingot cast from crucible-smelted native copper. Native copper requires no chemical reduction -- just heat (~1085C) to melt and pour.

tin_ingot.name: tin ingot
tin_ingot.description: Tin ingot smelted from cassiterite (SnO2) ore. Cassiterite requires carbon reduction at ~1100C (SnO2 + 2C -> Sn + 2CO2) before the metallic tin can be tapped and poured -- its low melting point of 232C only matters after reduction.

bronze_ingot.name: bronze ingot
bronze_ingot.description: Bronze ingot from a controlled copper-tin melt at 9:1 ratio -- approximately 10% tin by weight. Below ~6% tin gives little benefit; above ~17% the alloy becomes brittle. The 8-12% window is the historically proven sweet spot, documented from Bronze Age artifacts worldwide.

bronze_pickaxe.name: bronze pickaxe
bronze_pickaxe.description: A cast bronze pickaxe head hafted to a wooden handle. Cast from a stone ingot mold -- bronze tools predate the iron anvil by over a thousand years. Unlocks mining of oxide and sulfide ores.

roasted_ore_hematite.name: roasted hematite
roasted_ore_hematite.description: Hematite ore roasted in a pit to drive off moisture and volatile impurities, making it more amenable to reduction in a bloomery.

roasted_ore_bog_iron.name: roasted bog iron
roasted_ore_bog_iron.description: Bog iron roasted to drive off moisture and improve reduction quality in the bloomery.

iron_bloom.name: iron bloom
iron_bloom.description: A hot, spongy mass of iron and trapped slag pulled from a bloomery. Must be hammered to consolidate into usable wrought iron.

wrought_iron_bar.name: wrought iron bar
wrought_iron_bar.description: Iron bar produced by repeatedly heating and hammering a bloom to expel slag. The workhorse metal of the pre-industrial world.

iron_hammer_head.name: iron hammer head
iron_hammer_head.description: A wrought iron hammer head shaped on the anvil. Needs hafting to become a usable tool.

tool_iron_hammer.name: iron hammer
tool_iron_hammer.description: A wrought iron hammer for forge work and bloom consolidation. Required for efficient metalworking.

tool_geology_hammer.name: geology hammer
tool_geology_hammer.description: A small pointed hammer for chipping rock samples and identifying ore veins. Required to survey non-primitive ore nodes.

wrench_simple.name: simple wrench
wrench_simple.purpose: Configure pipes, conveyors, and machine links
wrench_simple.description: A drop-forged iron wrench used to bolt transporter segments to machine ports, flip flow direction, and toggle connections on each face. Held out, it lights up the per-face overlay on every transporter in view .

crushed_ore_hematite.name: crushed hematite
crushed_ore_hematite.description: Hematite ore crushed by stamp mill into fine fragments suitable for blast furnace or further separation.

crushed_ore_magnetite.name: crushed magnetite
crushed_ore_magnetite.description: Magnetite ore crushed by stamp mill; can be magnetically separated at higher tiers.

crushed_slag.name: crushed slag
crushed_slag.description: Blast furnace or bloomery slag crushed for secondary iron recovery. Contains trapped iron particles.

iron_tool_blank.name: iron tool blank
iron_tool_blank.description: Rough forged blank that can be shaped into tools like axes or picks.

clay_crucible_small.name: small clay crucible
clay_crucible_small.description: Thick-walled clay pot for melting small metal charges over charcoal.

bronze_tool_blank.name: bronze tool blank
bronze_tool_blank.description: Cast blank ready to be ground into fine tools.

bronze_pick.name: bronze pick
bronze_pick.description: Bronze pickaxe giving better durability than stone tools.

bronze_axe.name: bronze axe
bronze_axe.description: Bronze axe with higher durability and cutting speed than stone adze.

bronze_shovel.name: bronze shovel
bronze_shovel.description: Bronze shovel that moves more dirt and sand per swing than a crude shovel.

iron_strap.name: iron strap
iron_strap.description: Flat wrought-iron strap used to bind boilers, frames, and beams.

boiler_shell_riveted.name: riveted boiler shell
boiler_shell_riveted.purpose: Boil water with charcoal to make steam charges
boiler_shell_riveted.description: Cylindrical wrought-iron shell riveted from plates to form a low-pressure boiler. Place it, put water_raw in the input, charcoal in the fuel slot, and it produces steam_charge -- the portable unit of steam that powers the steam drill and other early machinery (placeholder until the proper fluid system arrives in E6/E7).

steam_charge.name: steam charge
steam_charge.description: A single charge of compressed steam. Boilers produce these from water + charcoal; the steam drill consumes one per output cycle. Stackable so a chute can buffer them between boiler and machine.

firebox_cast_iron.name: cast-iron firebox
firebox_cast_iron.description: Heavy cast-iron box that supports fuel bed and transfers heat to boiler shell.

steam_cylinder_cast_iron.name: steam cylinder (cast iron)
steam_cylinder_cast_iron.description: Bored cast-iron cylinder for a simple steam engine.

steam_piston_rod.name: steam piston and rod
steam_piston_rod.description: Piston, rod, and packing assembly that runs inside the cylinder.

steam_engine_simple.name: simple beam engine
steam_engine_simple.description: Single-acting beam engine driving a flywheel and line shaft via crank.

flyball_governor.name: flyball governor
flyball_governor.description: Spinning balls on arms adjust throttle to maintain roughly constant speed.

line_shaft_wrought.name: wrought-iron line shaft
line_shaft_wrought.description: A turning shaft that carries rotational power from one place to another. Lay it however you like to bring power where you need it.

flat_belt_leather.name: leather flat belt
flat_belt_leather.description: Joined leather strip used to couple line shafts and machinery.

belt_pulley_iron.name: cast-iron belt pulley
belt_pulley_iron.description: Keyed to a shaft to take off or pass along rotational power.

steam_pipe_wrought.name: wrought-iron steam pipe
steam_pipe_wrought.description: Riveted or welded pipe sections moving steam to engines and heaters.

steam_drill.name: steam drill
steam_drill.description: A steam-driven reciprocating drill placed ATOP an exposed ore node. While fed fuel from its hopper, it slowly chews the node down on its own and deposits raw ore into its output -- the first machine that mines without a player. One drill per node; outputs the same raw_ore_* items hand-mining produces. Output rate scales with the drill's DrillTier attribute (upgrade for more ore per tick).

condenser_jet.name: jet condenser
